big_picture:
The Indian government has slashed excise duty on petrol to Rs 3/litre and exempted diesel from the duty. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that the move is to not burden citizens. The decision comes amid global disruption in energy supplies. The government has also exempted diesel from the duty. 

Meghalaya Chief Minister has hailed Prime Minister Modi for his 'decisive leadership' in navigating challenges amid the West Asia conflict. The conflict has led to global disruption in energy supplies. The Prime Minister has spoken with US President Trump, discussing the importance of keeping the Strait of Hormuz open. 

Opposition leaders, including Kapil Sibal, have alleged 'gross misuse' of investigative agencies and called for a joint petition in the Supreme Court over the issue. Sibal stated that the Enforcement Directorate has turned into an 'endorsement directorate'.

articles.title: J&K&L High Court Orders MHA To Bring Back Minor Deported To Pakistan; Says Humanitarian Concerns Must Guide Sovereign Decisions
articles.summary: The Jammu & Kashmir High Court directed the Union Ministry of Home Affairs to take steps for retrieval of a minor boy who was deported to Pakistan and to consider his application for grant of Indian citizenship, emphasizing the need to balance sovereign powers with humanitarian considerations. articles.title: What insurance numbers do not reveal
articles.summary: The article examines how India's insurance penetration and density metrics—widely used to gauge insurance coverage—only reflect premium collection and not whether households are adequately protected against income loss. It argues that rapid premium growth is often misinterpreted as progress, even when financial safeguards remain weak. The analysis suggests current measures fail to capture the true effectiveness of insurance in shielding people from economic shocks.
articles.raw_summary: Insurance penetration and density are widely used measures, but they show premium collection, not how well households are protected against loss of income; as a result, premium growth is often mistaken for progress, even when financial protection remains limited

articles.summary: The case stemmed from the allocation in Maharashtra’s Bandar and led to the first CBI chargesheet in the larger coal block controversy dating back to the time Congress-led UPA was in office. articles.title: Credit-deposit ratio of banks touches a record 83%
articles.summary: Credit growth outpaced deposit mobilisation through the current financial year, with incremental credit at Rs 25.3 lakh crore exceeding incremental deposits of Rs 24.3 lakh crore. As a result, the incremental credit-deposit ratio stood at 103.9%. Historically, an 80% credit-deposit ratio is seen as healthy as it factors in the 3% of bank deposits that have to be maintained as cash reserves (CRR) and 18% in liquid govt bonds (statutory liquidity ratio)
